primaryText: If Kripke was there if Strawson was, then Anscomb was there
translation: If Kripke was there if Strawson was, then Anscomb was there
context: Said of a conference the addressee doesn't know much about.
comment: Gibbard's classic bare LNC, odd out of the blue (quoted with example number changed).

primaryText: If he is so unhappy, he should leave
translation: If he is so unhappy, he should leave
comment: PC echo of Alf's assertion, without the antecedent-uncertainty implication of HCs; anaphoric 'so' forces the PC reading.

primaryText: If Kripke was there if Strawson was, then Anscomb was there too
translation: If Kripke was there if Strawson was, then Anscomb was there too
context: Alf: Kripke was there if Strawson was.
comment: Gibbard's LNC improves dramatically when the antecedent echoes a previous assertion; a residual hint of oddity is attributed to an inference confound removed in (12).

primaryText: Aha. If Mary came if John did, they are friends
translation: Aha. If Mary came if John did, they are friends
context: Neither Alf nor Barbara was at the party. Alf: Mary came to the party if John did.
comment: Echoic LNC where the consequent is clearly inferable from the echoed conditional.

primaryText: If you'll cancel the camping trip if it rains, you're not from Scotland
translation: If you'll cancel the camping trip if it rains, you're not from Scotland
context: Carl: We'll cancel the camping trip if it rains.
comment: Draws an inference from Carl's expressed plan without doubting it.

primaryText: Given that/Since you'll cancel the camping trip if it rains, you're not from Scotland
translation: Given that/Since you'll cancel the camping trip if it rains, you're not from Scotland
context: Carl: We'll cancel the camping trip if it rains.
comment: Presuppositional 'given that'/'since' conveys approximately the same thought as the PC (13).

primaryText: Moshi meari-ga kita-{nara/ra} jon-ga kita-nara karera-wa tomodati-da
translation: If Mary came if John did, they are friends
comment: The main complementizer of a bare LNC can only be PC-compatible nara; the embedded conditional allows either marker.

primaryText: Moshi meari-ga kita-{nara/ra} jon-ga kita-ra karera-wa tomodati-da
translation: Intended: 'If Mary came if John did, they are friends'
comment: HC-only -ra is impossible as the main complementizer of a bare LNC.

primaryText: Wenn ihr das Spiel absagt, {wenn/falls} es regnet, seid ihr keine echten Göttinger
translation: If you cancel the picnic if it rains, you aren't real Göttingers
comment: General-purpose wenn is acceptable as the main complementizer of a LNC; the embedded conditional allows either marker.

primaryText: Falls ihr das Spiel absagt, {wenn/falls} es regnet, seid ihr keine echten Göttinger
translation: Intended: 'If you cancel the picnic if it rains, you aren't real Göttingers'
comment: HC-only falls is impossible as the main complementizer of a bare LNC.

primaryText: If Mary was rather pleased if Bill was involved, her expectations are too low
translation: If Mary was rather pleased if Bill was involved, her expectations are too low
comment: PPI in the consequent of the embedded conditional is fine — most naturally read as an echoic PC.

primaryText: If Mary lifted a finger if Bill was involved, the job got done
translation: If Mary lifted a finger if Bill was involved, the job got done
comment: NPI in the consequent of the embedded conditional is quite odd — it attempts to echo (31), which is unacceptable on its own.

primaryText: Mary lifted a finger if Bill was involved
translation: Mary lifted a finger if Bill was involved
comment: NPI in the consequent of a simple conditional is unlicensed; the echo target of (30).

primaryText: If Mary was pleased if Bill lifted a finger to help, her expectations are too low
translation: If Mary was pleased if Bill lifted a finger to help, her expectations are too low
comment: NPI acceptable in the antecedent of the embedded conditional, which can echo an HC with an NPI in its own antecedent (32a).

primaryText: If Bill is so unhappy, and if Mary loses her job, they'll be in trouble
translation: If Bill is so unhappy, and if Mary loses her job, they'll be in trouble
context: Ed and Fran have no idea whether Mary's job is at risk. Ed: Bill is very unhappy here.
comment: Coordinated antecedents cannot mix PC and HC interpretations (Haegeman & Schönenberger 2023: p.284ff.).

primaryText: If John came if Mary did, and if the food was bad, it was a lousy party
translation: If John came if Mary did, and if the food was bad, it was a lousy party
context: Alf: John came to the party if Mary did. Barbara: The food wasn't very good.
comment: Conjoined-antecedent LNC is acceptable when both conjuncts can be PCs in context.

primaryText: If John came if Mary did, and if the food was bad, it was a lousy party
translation: If John came if Mary did, and if the food was bad, it was a lousy party
context: Alf: John came to the party if Mary did. Barbara: I have no idea about the food — sometimes it's great, sometimes it's terrible.
comment: Same LNC degraded ('??') when the context favors an HC reading of the simple conjunct.

primaryText: Only if you have the courage to follow your heart will you succeed on the path of love
translation: Only if you have the courage to follow your heart will you succeed on the path of love
comment: HCs allow subject-aux inversion triggered by initial 'only' (Haegeman & Schönenberger 2023: p.288).

primaryText: Only if the chocolate manufacturers are pleased are they not satisfied
translation: Only if the chocolate manufacturers are pleased are they not satisfied
context: The chocolate manufacturers looked with pleasure at the statistics. If the chocolate manufacturers are pleased, they are not satisfied.
comment: The context forces a PC reading; 'only' + inversion removes the PC interpretation and the HC reading is nonsensical.

primaryText: If Mary was distressed if Bill left, Sue will be annoyed
translation: If Mary was distressed if Bill left, Sue will be annoyed
comment: Plain LNC counterpart of (38b).

primaryText: Only if Mary was distressed if Bill left will Sue be annoyed
translation: Only if Mary was distressed if Bill left will Sue be annoyed
comment: LNCs are unacceptable with initial 'only' and inversion, as predicted if they are PC-only.

primaryText: Only if the game is canceled if it rains are the organizers too nervous
translation: Only if the game is canceled if it rains are the organizers too nervous
comment: Second only-inversion LNC; the plain counterpart (39a) is acceptable.

primaryText: If the boss rarely yells at employees if they make a mistake, I'm going to be happier working here
translation: If the boss rarely yells at employees if they make a mistake, I'm going to be happier working here
context: I don't know how things work in my new workplace, but I hope people are kinder than in my previous one.
comment: Adverb of quantification in the embedded conditional makes an HC construal available.

primaryText: If Bill is obliged to stop writing if the timer has gone off, he's breaking the rules by continuing
translation: If Bill is obliged to stop writing if the timer has gone off, he's breaking the rules by continuing
context: I don't know what the rules of this test are, but it seems like something's not right here.
comment: Modal in the embedded conditional makes an HC construal available.

primaryText: If a female bear attacks if a human approaches, she has cubs
translation: If a female bear attacks if a human approaches, she has cubs
comment: Generic/habitual construal of the embedded conditional makes an HC construal available.

primaryText: If this switch will fail if it is submerged in water, it will be discarded
translation: If this switch will fail if it is submerged in water, it will be discarded
comment: From Kaufmann 2009: p.18, as cited by Lassiter; 'will' is a modal describing a disposition of the switch.

primaryText: If this material becomes soft if it gets hot, it is not suited for our purposes
translation: If this material becomes soft if it gets hot, it is not suited for our purposes
comment: From Douven 2016, as cited by Lassiter; generic interpretation of the material's tendency (cf. Lassiter & Baratgin 2021).
